title: Bridge Essay: Intrusive thinking: Circuit and synaptic mechanisms of a transdiagnostic psychiatric symptom.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.

**Intrusive thinking: Circuit and synaptic mechanisms of a transdiagnostic psychiatric symptom.** — Neurosci Biobehav Rev (2023). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2023.105196. PMID: 37094741. Spontaneous thought is an adaptive cognitive process that can produce novel and insightful thought sequences useful in guiding future behavior. In many psychiatric disorders, spontaneous thinking becomes intrusive and uncontrolled, and can trigger symptoms such as craving, repetitive negative thinking and trauma-related memories.
